behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction lists exact strings for spawn call, error message, command description, option description, and test file updates. It names the files (cli/src/index.ts, cli/src/index.test.ts), the function runKanbanAlias, and all literal strings that tests assert on. The test for 'runKanbanAlias' in content is a pass-to-pass check on pre-existing code. All asserted specifics are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact replacement strings: e.g., 'The code that spawns the npx process must use the arguments ["-y", "kanban@latest", "--agent", "cline"]', 'error message must reference "npx kanban@latest --agent cline"', etc. This tells the agent exactly WHAT to change and TO WHAT, leaving no reasoning required.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use exact substring matching like 'spawn(getNpxCommand(), ["-y", "kanban@latest", "--agent", "cline"]' and '.description("Run npx kanban@latest --agent cline")'. An alternative correct fix that uses different formatting (e.g., single quotes, different spacing, template literals) would fail despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_kanban_spawn_uses_latest_tag, test_kanban_error_message_updated, test_kanban_command_description_updated, test_kanban_option_description_updated, test_test_file_consistent) all exclusively use 'assert ... in content' pattern reading file contents as text. No test actually executes the CLI or calls any function.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check for specific string changes (kanban@latest) and also assert the old strings (kanban without @latest) are NOT present. A stub or unchanged file would fail because the original file contains the old strings.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The agent can read /workspace/cline/tests/test_outputs.py or the solution directory (solve.sh) to see exact expected strings. Additionally, since tests just check for literal strings in files, the agent could grep test files for fixture values and write them directly without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five required changes listed in instruction.md (spawn call, error message, command description, option description, test file consistency) are each covered by dedicated test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_kanban_spawn_uses_latest_tag, test_kanban_error_message_updated, etc.), have docstrings indicating fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass, and are logically grouped by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves editing source code string literals, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction provides exact strings to use including the precise spawn arguments, error message text, .description() and .option() call formats. This goes beyond naming the buggy file/module and essentially gives the exact code changes needed.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits/config task.
